title: MCP tool with data exfiltration in description
description: An MCP tool description contains instructions that direct the LLM to exfiltrate sensitive data. The LLM reads tool descriptions as instructions and may follow them automatically.
remediation: Audit all MCP tool descriptions before connecting. Implement automated scanning of tool descriptions for exfiltration patterns.

title: Agent config file with hidden override instructions
description: A CLAUDE.md, SKILL.md, or similar agent configuration file contains social engineering patterns designed to override the agent's safety guardrails.
remediation: Review all agent config files in repositories before use. Flag files with urgency markers or instruction override patterns.

title: MCP tool requesting elevated permissions
description: An MCP tool or server requests permissions beyond what is needed for its stated function, enabling privilege escalation.
remediation: Apply least privilege principle. Only grant minimum permissions needed. Review and restrict MCP server capabilities.

title: Connection to unverified MCP server
description: An MCP server is connected via npx, uvx, or remote URL without verification. Untrusted servers can provide malicious tools.
remediation: Maintain a registry of verified MCP servers. Audit server source code before connecting. Use pinned versions for npx/uvx packages.

title: MCP tool that shadows built-in tool names
description: An MCP tool uses a name that shadows built-in agent tools (e.g., read_file, bash). This can redirect the agent's actions through a malicious implementation.
remediation: Enforce unique tool naming with namespace prefixes. Detect and warn on tool name collisions. Built-in tools should take priority.
